description: Capacitor (polarized) — electrolytic or tantalum, bulk decoupling, power supply filtering

pins.description: Positive terminal — must connect to higher potential
pins.requirements: Always connect + to positive rail — reverse polarity destroys the capacitor and can cause it to explode

pins.description: Negative terminal — connect to GND or lower potential

common_mistakes: NEVER reverse polarity — electrolytic capacitors explode when reverse biased
Voltage rating must exceed circuit voltage by at least 20%
Tantalum caps can fail short-circuit — use with current-limiting series resistor in sensitive circuits
ESR (Equivalent Series Resistance) matters in power supply filtering — use low-ESR types
notes: Value in Farads (F). Common values: 10µF, 47µF, 100µF, 470µF. Always mark + terminal clearly on schematic.
